name: Workflow 3 – P2P Report: Send to Admin Telegram

nodes.name: Webhook – Receive Send Request
nodes.notes:
Called by Node.js after it looks up the admin's telegramChatId.
Expects JSON body: { chat_id, admin_name, team_name, subteam_name, sender_name, report_markdown }

nodes.name: Send Header Message
nodes.notes:
n8n Cloud setup:
  Settings → Variables → Add:
    Name: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN
    Value: your bot token from @BotFather

parameters.responseBody: ={ "success": true, "message": "Report sent to admin on Telegram" }

n8n_cloud_variables.how_to_set: n8n Cloud → Settings → Variables → Add Variable

required.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN: Your bot token from @BotFather on Telegram

_setup_guide.webhook_url:
Production webhook URL: https://amrfawzy26.app.n8n.cloud/webhook/send-telegram-report

Set this as N8N_TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL in Node-js/.env
_setup_guide.message_format:
Admin receives two Telegram messages:
1. Header: who sent it, from which team/subteam
2. Report body: up to 4000 chars (truncated with a note if longer)
_setup_guide.how_it_works: No Code node HTTP calls — uses native HTTP Request nodes only. Format Messages (Code) prepares the text, then two HTTP Request nodes send header and report sequentially to Telegram.
